# VELLORE INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY, CHENNAI

## SCHOOL OF COMPUTER SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING (SCOPE)
### CONTINUOUS ASSESSMENT TEST - I
### FALL SEMESTER 2026-27

---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Programme Name & Branch** | B.Tech (CSE) |
| **Course Code and Course Name** | BCSE302L – Database Systems |
| **Slot** | — |
| **Class Number(s)** | — |
| **Faculty Name(s)** | — |
| **Date of Examination** | — |
| **Exam Duration** | 90 minutes |
| **Maximum Marks** | **50** |

---

### General Instructions:
- Answer **ALL** questions.
- M – Max mark; CO – Course Outcome; BL – Bloom's Taxonomy Level  
  (1 – Remember, 2 – Understand, 3 – Apply, 4 – Analyse, 5 – Evaluate, 6 – Create)

### Course Outcomes:
- **CO1** – Understand the fundamentals of database systems and data models
- **CO2** – Apply relational algebra and SQL for data manipulation
- **CO3** – Analyse functional dependencies and apply normalization techniques

---

### Answer ALL Questions (5 × 10 = 50 Marks)

| Q. No | Question | M | CO | BL |
|:-----:|----------|:-:|:--:|:--:|
| 1. | **(a)** Explain the three-schema architecture of a database system with a neat diagram. Discuss how data independence is achieved at each level. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Differentiate between database schema and database state with suitable examples. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL2 |
| 2. | Consider the following university database: | | | |
| | **STUDENT** (Roll_No, Name, DOB, Dept_Name) | | | |
| | **COURSE** (Course_ID, Course_Name, Credits, Dept_Name) | | | |
| | **ENROLLMENT** (Roll_No, Course_ID, Semester, Grade) | | | |
| | **DEPARTMENT** (Dept_Name, HOD_Name, Building) | | | |
| | **(a)** Draw the ER diagram for the above schema showing entities, attributes (identifying primary keys and partial keys), relationships with cardinality constraints, and participation constraints. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Extend the above ER diagram to an EER diagram by introducing: (i) a specialization of STUDENT into UG_STUDENT and PG_STUDENT with appropriate attributes, and (ii) a category (union type) combining STUDENT and DEPARTMENT as SCHOLARSHIP_HOLDER. Show disjoint/overlapping and total/partial constraints. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL3 |
| 3. | Consider the relations: | | | |
| | **EMPLOYEE** (Emp_ID, Name, Dept_ID, Salary, Manager_ID) | | | |
| | **DEPARTMENT** (Dept_ID, Dept_Name, Location) | | | |
| | **PROJECT** (Proj_ID, Proj_Name, Dept_ID, Budget) | | | |
| | **WORKS_ON** (Emp_ID, Proj_ID, Hours) | | | |
| | Write **Relational Algebra** expressions for the following queries: | | | |
| | **(a)** Find the names of employees who work on ALL projects controlled by the 'Research' department. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** List the department names where every employee earns a salary greater than 50,000. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** Find the names and salaries of employees who do NOT work on any project with a budget exceeding 1,00,000. **(4 Marks)** | 10 | CO2 | BL4 |
| 4. | **(a)** Using the same schema as Q3, write SQL queries for the following: | | | |
| | (i) Retrieve the department name and the average salary of employees for departments having more than 5 employees, ordered by average salary in descending order. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Create a view `HIGH_PERFORMERS` that lists employee names who work more than 20 hours on any single project, along with the project name. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Explain with examples: (i) the difference between `HAVING` and `WHERE` clauses, and (ii) the difference between `DELETE`, `DROP`, and `TRUNCATE` commands in SQL. **(4 Marks)** | 10 | CO2 | BL3 |
| 5. | **(a)** Consider the relation R(A, B, C, D, E, F) with the following set of functional dependencies: | | | |
| | F = { A → BC, CD → E, B → D, E → A, D → F } | | | |
| | (i) Find the candidate keys of R. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Determine whether R is in 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, or BCNF. Justify your answer at each level. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Decompose R into BCNF relations. Verify that your decomposition is lossless and dependency preserving. **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO3 | BL4 |

---

| 4. | **(a)** Explain log-based recovery using the **Write-Ahead Logging (WAL)** protocol. Describe the purpose of UNDO and REDO operations during recovery. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Consider the following log sequence with a system crash: | | | |

| | Using the ARIES-style recovery (or immediate modification with checkpointing), determine: (i) Which transactions need to be undone? (ii) Which transactions need to be redone? (iii) What are the final values of A, B, C, D after recovery? **(6 Marks)** | 10 | CO5 | BL4 |

| | **(b)** Discuss the roles of DBA and end-users in a database environment. Explain the different types of database users with their interactions. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL2 |
| 2. | A hospital management system has the following requirements: | | | |
| | - A **DOCTOR** has Doctor_ID, Name, Specialization, and Phone. | | | |
| | - A **PATIENT** has Patient_ID, Name, DOB, Address, and Blood_Group. | | | |
| | - A **WARD** has Ward_No, Ward_Type, and Capacity. | | | |
| | - Doctors treat patients; each treatment has a Date and Diagnosis. | | | |
| | - Patients are admitted to wards with an Admission_Date. | | | |
| | - A doctor can be a **Surgeon** (with Surgery_Count) or a **Physician** (with Clinic_Hours). | | | |
| | - A **NURSE** assists doctors and is assigned to wards. | | | |
| | **(a)** Draw a complete EER diagram with: entities, attributes (key, multivalued, derived, composite), relationships with cardinality and participation constraints, specialization/generalization with constraints. **(7 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Map the EER diagram to a relational schema. List all relations with their attributes, primary keys, and foreign keys. **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL3 |
| 3. | Consider the following relations: | | | |
| | **SUPPLIER** (Sid, Sname, City) | | | |
| | **PARTS** (Pid, Pname, Color, Weight) | | | |
| | **CATALOG** (Sid, Pid, Cost) | | | |
| | Write **Relational Algebra** expressions for: | | | |
| | **(a)** Find the names of suppliers who supply every red part. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Find the Sid of suppliers who supply at least two different parts each costing more than ₹500. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | Write **Tuple Relational Calculus** expressions for: | | | |
| | **(c)** Find the names of suppliers who supply some part that is also supplied by supplier S2. **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(d)** Find the Pid of parts supplied by ALL suppliers from 'Chennai'. **(2 Marks)** | 10 | CO2 | BL4 |
| 4. | Using the SUPPLIER-PARTS-CATALOG schema from Q3, write SQL queries for: | | | |
| | **(a)** Find the names of suppliers who supply every part that costs less than ₹1000. (Use NOT EXISTS) **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** For each city, find the supplier who offers the maximum number of distinct parts. Display city, supplier name, and part count. **(3 Marks)** | | | |

| | (i) Find all candidate keys. **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Is R in BCNF? If not, decompose into BCNF. Check if the decomposition is dependency-preserving. Discuss the trade-off between BCNF and dependency preservation. **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO3 | BL5 |
| 7. | **(a)** Construct a B+ tree of order 5 by inserting the following keys: **10, 20, 5, 15, 25, 30, 35, 40, 3, 8, 12, 18, 22, 28, 33**. Show the final tree structure. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Explain the following indexing concepts with examples: | | | |
| | (i) Primary Index vs. Secondary Index **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Dense Index vs. Sparse Index **(1 Mark)** | | | |
| | (iii) Multilevel Indexing and its advantages over single-level indexing **(2 Marks)** | 10 | CO4 | BL3 |
| 8. | **(a)** Given the following schedule of five transactions, determine if it is conflict-serializable using the precedence graph method: | | | |
| | `S: R1(A), R2(B), W3(A), R4(A), W1(B), W2(A), R3(B), W4(B), R5(A), W5(B)` | | | |
| | If serializable, provide the equivalent serial order(s). If not, explain why. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Two transactions T1 and T2 operate under the **Wait-Die** and **Wound-Wait** deadlock prevention schemes. TS(T1) = 10, TS(T2) = 15. Explain what happens in each scheme when: | | | |
| | (i) T1 requests a lock held by T2 | | | |
| | (ii) T2 requests a lock held by T1 **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO5 | BL4 |
| 9. | **(a)** Compare and contrast the following concurrency control protocols: | | | |
| | (i) Two-Phase Locking (2PL) vs. Timestamp Ordering Protocol **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Optimistic Concurrency Control – explain its three phases (Read, Validation, Write) with an example. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** What is a deadlock in database systems? Explain deadlock detection using a Wait-For graph with an example. Describe one deadlock prevention technique in detail. **(4 Marks)** | 10 | CO5 | BL4 |
| 10. | **(a)** Explain the ARIES recovery algorithm. Describe its three phases: Analysis, Redo, and Undo. How does it use LSN (Log Sequence Number) and the dirty page table? **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Consider the following log with a system crash: | | | |
| | ``` | | | |
| | <T1, Start> | | | |
| | <T1, A, 1000, 2000> | | | |
| | <T2, Start> | | | |
| | <T2, B, 3000, 4000> | | | |
| | <T1, Commit> | | | |
| | <CHECKPOINT {T2}> | | | |
| | <T3, Start> | | | |
| | <T2, C, 5000, 6000> | | | |
| | <T3, D, 7000, 8000> | | | |
| | <T2, Commit> | | | |
| | <T3, E, 9000, 1000> | | | |
| | *** SYSTEM CRASH *** | | | |
| | ``` | | | |
| | Apply the recovery procedure. State which transactions are redone and which are undone. What are the final values of A, B, C, D, E? **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO5 | BL4 |

| | (i) Find the order of elements 3, 5, and 10. **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Find all subgroups generated by elements of order 5. **(1 Mark)** | | | |
| | **(c)** Prove that every group of prime order is cyclic. **(2 Marks)** | 10 | CO3 | BL4 |
| 4. | **(a)** Let R = (ℤ₁₀, +₁₀, ×₁₀). | | | |
| | (i) Show that R is a commutative ring with unity. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Find all zero divisors and all units of ℤ₁₀. **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (iii) Is ℤ₁₀ an integral domain? A field? Justify. **(1 Mark)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Define Ring Homomorphism. Let φ: ℤ → ℤ₄ be defined by φ(n) = n mod 4. Prove that φ is a ring homomorphism and find its kernel. **(4 Marks)** | 10 | CO3 | BL4 |
| 5. | **(a)** In how many ways can 8 people be seated around a circular table if 3 specific people must NOT sit next to each other? **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Using the Pigeonhole Principle, prove that in any group of 25 people, at least 3 of them have birthdays in the same month. **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** How many solutions are there to x₁ + x₂ + x₃ + x₄ = 20, where xᵢ are non-negative integers and x₁ ≤ 6, x₂ ≤ 7? Use Inclusion-Exclusion. **(4 Marks)** | 10 | CO4 | BL4 |
| 6. | **(a)** Solve the recurrence relation: | | | |
| | **aₙ − 6aₙ₋₁ + 9aₙ₋₂ = 0**, with a₀ = 1, a₁ = 6 **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Solve using generating functions: | | | |
| | **aₙ = 2aₙ₋₁ + 1**, with a₀ = 0. Find a closed-form expression for aₙ. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** Find the number of binary strings of length n that do NOT contain "00" as a substring. Set up and solve the recurrence relation. **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO4 | BL3 |
| 7. | **(a)** Define: (i) Euler circuit and Euler path, (ii) Hamiltonian circuit and Hamiltonian path. State the necessary and sufficient conditions for the existence of each. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** For the following graph G with vertices {A, B, C, D, E} and edges {AB, AC, AD, BC, BD, CD, CE, DE}: | | | |
| | (i) Determine if G has an Euler circuit, Euler path, or neither. Justify. **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Determine if G has a Hamiltonian circuit. If yes, find one. If no, justify. **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** State and prove that the Petersen graph is NOT Hamiltonian. **(2 Marks)** | 10 | CO5 | BL4 |
| 8. | **(a)** Define proper graph coloring and chromatic number. Find the chromatic number of: | | | |
| | (i) The complete graph K₅ **(1 Mark)** | | | |
| | (ii) The complete bipartite graph K₃,₃ **(1 Mark)** | | | |
| | (iii) The cycle C₇ **(1 Mark)** | | | |
| | (iv) The Petersen graph **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** State and prove the Five-Color Theorem (or state the Four-Color Theorem and discuss its significance). **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** Using Kuratowski's theorem, determine whether the following graph is planar: A graph G with 6 vertices where each vertex has degree 4. **(2 Marks)** | 10 | CO5 | BL4 |
| 9. | **(a)** Define a tree. Prove that a tree with n vertices has exactly (n−1) edges. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Use **Kruskal's algorithm** to find the minimum spanning tree for the following weighted graph: | | | |
| | Vertices: {A, B, C, D, E, F} | | | |
| | Edges with weights: AB=2, AC=3, AD=7, BC=4, BD=5, BE=6, CE=1, CF=8, DE=3, DF=4, EF=5 | | | |

| | **(c)** Find the minimum spanning tree using **Prim's algorithm** starting from vertex A for the same graph. Compare the result with Kruskal's. **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO5 | BL3 |
| 10. | **(a)** State Euler's formula for connected planar graphs and verify it for the following: | | | |
| | (i) K₄ **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) K₂,₃ **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Prove that K₃,₃ is not planar using the corollary of Euler's formula (e ≤ 2v − 4 for bipartite graphs). **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** A connected graph G has 10 vertices. Each vertex has degree 3. | | | |
| | (i) Find the number of edges in G. **(1 Mark)** | | | |

---

### General Instructions:
- Answer **ALL** questions.
- M – Max mark; CO – Course Outcome; BL – Bloom's Taxonomy Level  
  (1 – Remember, 2 – Understand, 3 – Apply, 4 – Analyse, 5 – Evaluate, 6 – Create)

### Course Outcomes:
- **CO3** – Analyze, identify and select suitable types of virtualization
- **CO4** – An ability to use techniques, tools, skills in a secured cloud environment

---

### Answer ALL Questions (5 × 10 = 50 Marks)

| Q. No | Question | M | CO | BL |
|:-----:|----------|:-:|:--:|:--:|
| 1. | **(a)** Consider a scenario where there are three machines (Machine 1, Machine 2, and Machine 3) working on a task set. The tasks assigned to each machine have specific execution times in seconds: | | | |
| | - **Machine 1**: Task 1 = 5s, Task 2 = 8s, Task 3 = 3s | | | |
| | - **Machine 2**: Task 1 = 9s, Task 2 = 4s, Task 3 = 12s | | | |
| | - **Machine 3**: Task 1 = 6s, Task 2 = 7s, Task 3 = 10s | | | |
| | Using the **MAX-MIN Scheduling Algorithm**, compute the overall execution time for all tasks across all machines. Show all iterations clearly. **(5 Marks)** | 5 | CO3 | BL3 |
| | **(b)** Consider 8 tasks (Task 1 to Task 8) with lengths of 8000 MI, 6000 MI, 200 MI, 1500 MI, 7000 MI, 4000 MI, 9000 MI, and 3000 MI respectively, and corresponding execution times of 4s, 3s, 0.1s, 1.5s, 3.5s, 2s, 4.5s, and 1.5s. There are three Virtual Machines (VMs) with processing speeds of 800 MIPS, 1200 MIPS, and 2000 MIPS. Apply the **Shortest Job First (SJF)** scheduling algorithm to allocate the tasks to the VMs. Calculate the total waiting time for all tasks and determine how tasks are distributed across the VMs. **(5 Marks)** | 5 | CO3 | BL3 |
| 2. | **MediCare Solutions** is a growing chain of 15 hospitals and 50 clinics across South India. They currently store all patient records (including medical history, lab reports, imaging data, and billing) on local servers at each facility. They face the following challenges: | | | |
| | - Doctors at one hospital cannot access patient records from another facility | | | |
| | - The company must comply with India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA) 2023 | | | |
| | - They experience 3x traffic surges during seasonal disease outbreaks | | | |
| | - Their annual IT budget is limited to ₹2 crore | | | |
| | **(a)** What are the primary challenges that MediCare faces in migrating to the cloud? Discuss at least 4 specific challenges related to data security, compliance, interoperability, and cost. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Propose a detailed cloud migration strategy for MediCare. Specify the deployment model, service model, data migration approach (lift-and-shift vs. re-architecting), and security measures. Justify each recommendation. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO4 | BL4 |
| 3. | **(a)** Explain the **MapReduce programming model** with a neat diagram. Describe the Map phase, Shuffle/Sort phase, and Reduce phase. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Consider a large e-commerce company that needs to analyze 1 TB of daily transaction logs to find: (i) the top 10 most purchased products, and (ii) the total revenue per city. Write the pseudo-code for the Map and Reduce functions for EACH of these analyses. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO4 | BL3 |
| 4. | **(a)** Compare **Virtual Machines** and **Containers** in terms of: architecture, resource overhead, startup time, isolation level, portability, and use cases. Use a diagram to illustrate the architectural differences. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Explain the key components of **Docker** architecture (Docker Client, Docker Daemon, Docker Registry, Docker Images, Docker Containers). Write a sample Dockerfile for deploying a Python Flask web application and explain each instruction. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO3 | BL3 |
| 5. | **(a)** Define a **Service Level Agreement (SLA)** in cloud computing. Explain the key components of an SLA including: service scope, performance metrics, availability guarantees (e.g., 99.99% uptime), penalties, and responsibilities. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Explain different **load balancing algorithms** used in cloud computing: Round Robin, Weighted Round Robin, Least Connections, and IP Hash. For each, describe when it is most appropriate and its limitations. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** A cloud provider guarantees 99.95% uptime in their SLA. Calculate: (i) the maximum allowed downtime per month (in minutes), and (ii) if the actual downtime was 45 minutes in a month, determine if the SLA was violated and what the penalty credit should be (given the contract specifies 10% credit per 0.1% below SLA). **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO4 | BL4 |

---

> **⚡ PREDICTION PAPER** – Based on actual VIT CAT-2 papers from CSI3001 (Oct 2024) and Cloud Computing Methodologies (Feb 2025). Pattern: Q1 always has MAX-MIN + SJF scheduling, Q2 is scenario-based healthcare/enterprise migration, remaining questions on MapReduce, containers, and SLAs.

---

*END OF QUESTION PAPER*

### Answer ALL Questions (10 × 10 = 100 Marks)

| | **(b)** Compare the four cloud deployment models — Public, Private, Hybrid, and Community — with a table covering: control, cost, security, scalability, and example use cases. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL2 |
| 2. | **(a)** Draw and explain the NIST Cloud Computing Reference Architecture, detailing all five actors and their interactions. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** A large national bank with 500 branches wants to move its core banking application to the cloud. They handle sensitive financial data, must comply with RBI guidelines, and need 24/7 availability. Recommend a cloud strategy (deployment model + service model) and justify your choice. Discuss at least 3 security measures they must implement. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL4 |
| 3. | **(a)** Explain the differences between IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS using the "Pizza as a Service" analogy. For each model, name two real-world cloud providers and their specific offerings. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** An EdTech startup is building a mobile learning app with video streaming, real-time quizzes, and AI-based personalized learning paths. They have 5 developers and no DevOps team. | | | |
| | (i) Which cloud service model is most suitable? Justify. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Design a cloud architecture diagram showing how the app components (frontend, backend, database, AI engine, CDN for video) would be deployed using your recommended service model. **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO2 | BL4 |
| 4. | **(a)** Explain the concept of virtualization in cloud computing. Discuss the following types of virtualization with examples: | | | |
| | (i) Server Virtualization | | | |
| | (ii) Storage Virtualization | | | |
| | (iii) Network Virtualization | | | |
| | (iv) Desktop Virtualization **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Compare Type-1 and Type-2 hypervisors with a neat diagram. Discuss the concept of **paravirtualization** vs. **full virtualization** and when each approach is preferred. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO3 | BL3 |
| 5. | **(a)** Consider 10 tasks with the following MI (Million Instructions) and execution times: | | | |
| | | Task | T1 | T2 | T3 | T4 | T5 | T6 | T7 | T8 | T9 | T10 | | | | |
| | |------|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|------| | | | |
| | | MI | 12000 | 5000 | 8000 | 3000 | 15000 | 7000 | 2000 | 9000 | 6000 | 11000 | | | | |
| | Four VMs with processing speeds: VM1=1000 MIPS, VM2=2000 MIPS, VM3=1500 MIPS, VM4=3000 MIPS. | | | |
| | (i) Allocate tasks using **MAX-MIN Scheduling**. Show each iteration. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Allocate the same tasks using **MIN-MIN Scheduling**. Compare the makespan with MAX-MIN. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO3 | BL3 |
| 6. | **(a)** Explain the MapReduce programming model. Describe the complete data flow from input to output with a diagram showing: Input Splits → Map → Combine → Shuffle/Sort → Reduce → Output. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** A social media platform wants to analyze 500 GB of daily user activity logs to find: | | | |
| | (i) The top 5 trending hashtags **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Average session duration per country **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | Write the Map and Reduce pseudo-code for each analysis. | 10 | CO4 | BL3 |
| 7. | **(a)** Explain the evolution from **Physical Servers → Virtual Machines → Containers → Serverless Computing**. For each stage, discuss advantages and limitations. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Discuss Docker architecture in detail. Write a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Node.js application that: (i) builds the application, (ii) runs tests, (iii) creates a production image. Explain each instruction. **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** Compare Docker and Kubernetes. Explain how Kubernetes orchestrates containers using: Pods, Services, Deployments, and ReplicaSets. **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO3 | BL3 |
| 8. | **(a)** Discuss the major **cloud security threats** (as per CSA's Egregious Eleven or similar framework). Explain at least 5 threats with examples and mitigation strategies. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Explain the following cloud security mechanisms: | | | |
| | (i) Identity and Access Management (IAM) **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Data encryption at rest and in transit **(1.5 Marks)** | | | |
| | (iii) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) **(1.5 Marks)** | 10 | CO4 | BL3 |
| 9. | **(a)** Define Service Level Agreement (SLA). Explain the SLA life cycle. Discuss the role of SLA in ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) between cloud provider and consumer. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Discuss the different **cloud storage types**: Block Storage, Object Storage, and File Storage. Compare them in terms of performance, use cases, cost, and scalability. Provide real-world examples (e.g., AWS S3, EBS, EFS). **(3 Marks)** | | | |

| | (i) Maximum allowed downtime per year in minutes **(1 Mark)** | | | |
| | (ii) If 100 million objects are stored, how many objects could potentially be lost in a year? **(1 Mark)** | | | |
| | (iii) If actual availability was 99.95%, determine the penalty (SLA credits at 25% per 0.01% below threshold). **(1 Mark)** | 10 | CO4 | BL4 |
| 10. | **Case Study: GreenHarvest AgriTech** | | | |
| | GreenHarvest is an agricultural technology company that uses IoT sensors in farms across 10 states to collect real-time data on soil moisture, temperature, humidity, and crop health. They process 50 GB of sensor data daily and use machine learning models to predict crop yields and suggest optimal irrigation schedules. Currently they run everything on 20 on-premise servers. | | | |

| | **(a)** Design a comprehensive cloud migration plan for GreenHarvest. Specify: deployment model, service models for each component, data pipeline architecture, and security measures. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Propose a cloud architecture diagram showing how IoT data flows from sensors → cloud ingestion → processing (real-time & batch) → ML model training → dashboard. Recommend specific cloud services (from any major provider). **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** How would you handle the challenges of: (i) real-time processing, (ii) seasonal scaling, (iii) low-bandwidth mobile access? **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO4 | BL5 |

| | **(b)** Explain the salient features of the Indian Constitution that make it unique among the world's constitutions. Discuss at least 6 features including: written constitution, federal with unitary bias, parliamentary form, blend of rigidity and flexibility, secularism, and independent judiciary. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL2 |
| 2. | **(a)** Explain the **Preamble** of the Indian Constitution. Discuss the significance of the following key terms used in the Preamble: Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic, Justice, Liberty, Equality, and Fraternity. **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** "The Preamble is the key to the Constitution." Critically analyze this statement with reference to the **Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala (1973)** and **S.R. Bommai v. Union of India (1994)** cases. Can the Preamble be amended? Discuss with reference to the 42nd Amendment. **(5 Marks)** | 10 | CO1 | BL4 |
| 3. | **(a)** Explain the **Right to Equality** (Articles 14-18) in detail. Discuss: | | | |
| | (i) Article 14 – Equality before law and equal protection of laws. How is "reasonable classification" applied by the courts? **(3 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Article 15 – Prohibition of discrimination. What are the exceptions allowed under Articles 15(3), 15(4), and 15(5)? **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (iii) Article 16 – Equality of opportunity in public employment and the concept of reservation. **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Explain Articles 17 (Abolition of Untouchability) and 18 (Abolition of Titles) with relevant case laws. **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO2 | BL3 |
| 4. | **(a)** Discuss the **Right to Freedom** (Article 19) in detail. Explain the six freedoms guaranteed under Article 19(1) and the reasonable restrictions that can be imposed by the State under Articles 19(2) to 19(6). **(5 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Explain the following with landmark judgments: | | | |
| | (i) Article 20 – Protection against ex-post facto laws and double jeopardy **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Article 21 – Right to Life and Personal Liberty. Discuss the expanded interpretation of Article 21 through cases like **Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India (1978)** and the right to privacy (**K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India, 2017**). **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO2 | BL4 |
| 5. | **(a)** Explain the **Right against Exploitation** (Articles 23-24). Discuss: | | | |
| | (i) Article 23 – Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labor **(2 Marks)** | | | |
| | (ii) Article 24 – Prohibition of employment of children in factories **(1 Mark)** | | | |
| | **(b)** Discuss the **Right to Freedom of Religion** (Articles 25-28). How does the Constitution balance religious freedom with social reform? Discuss with reference to the concept of "essential religious practices" and the Triple Talaq judgment. **(4 Marks)** | | | |
| | **(c)** Explain **Article 32 – Right to Constitutional Remedies**. Why did Dr. B.R. Ambedkar call it "the heart and soul of the Constitution"? Describe the five types of writs. **(3 Marks)** | 10 | CO2 | BL3 |
